message:
fix(http2): mitigate CVE-2023-44487 Rapid Reset attack with RST_STREAM rate limiting

Add per-connection rate limiting to prevent attackers from rapidly opening
and resetting streams to exhaust server resources. Implements a sliding
30-second window allowing maximum 100 RST_STREAM frames; additional
frames trigger ENHANCE_YOUR_CALM connection error per RFC 7540 §6.9.

Also add automatic pruning of closed streams when the stream map grows
beyond 2x the configured max_concurrent_streams, preventing unbounded
memory growth on long-lived connections under churn workloads.

Changes:
- Add RST_STREAM_RATE_LIMIT (100) and RST_STREAM_RATE_WINDOW_MS (30000)
- Track per-connection rst_stream_count and rst_stream_window_start
- Validate rate limit in process_rst_stream() with window reset logic
- Prune closed streams in main frame processing loop before returning
- Add StreamStore::len() and is_empty() introspection methods
- Add test: rst_stream_flood_triggers_enhance_your_calm()
- Update stream store allocation test with empty/non-empty assertions

References: RFC 7540 §6.4 (RST_STREAM), §6.9 (GOAWAY), CVE-2023-44487

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
